name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Your AI agent keeps rebuilding decisions your team already made. Here's the fix I landed on.
articles.summary: Coding agents have no memory of why your codebase is the way it is. They re-derive settled decisions every session, confidently redo things your team already ruled out, and burn tokens doing it. I tried the three obvious fixes (a CLAUDE.md file, RAG over our docs, pasting context manually) and each...
articles.author: Ali Abbas

articles.title: My MCP server had 32 green tests. Not one of them had ever called a tool.
articles.summary: For weeks my CI badge was green and I believed it. Safari MCP is an open-source tool that lets an AI coding agent drive a real, logged-in Safari — click, type, read the page, switch tabs. It registers 96 tools. The test suite ran on every push across three Node versions and came back 32 passed, 0...
articles.author: אחיה כהן

articles.title: Connect a Laravel AI Agent to Any MCP Server: A Hands-On Guide
articles.summary: Originally published at hafiz.dev Most Laravel MCP tutorials teach you to build a server. You expose your app's tools, an AI client connects, and Claude or Cursor can query your orders table. Useful, and I've covered building an MCP server with Filament before. articles.title: Who gets to decide what the agentic economy becomes?
articles.summary: This isn't an explainer. It's a proposal, and an invitation. Here is the thing almost nobody says out loud: the agentic economy is not a future event we are waiting for. It is being designed right now, in ordinary engineering decisions that almost no one is voting on. When one company decides how...

articles.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) for Developers: From Figma Design to Code in Minutes
articles.summary: If you've watched developers build an entire React page from a Figma design in minutes, you've probably heard them mention MCP. It has quickly become one of the most talked about tools in AI-assisted development, especially for frontend engineers. This guide explains what MCP is, why it matters in...

articles.title: How I Found a Silent API Bug in a 3,548-Star MCP Server
articles.summary: How I Found a Silent API Bug in a 3,548-Star MCP Server The API accepted your request. Returned HTTP 200. And then quietly did nothing. No error. No stack trace. articles.title: Adding more Claude subagents made my pipeline slower — here's the specific reason why
articles.summary: Scaling from 4 to 8 Claude Code subagents pushed my error rate from 0.8% to 4.3%. The bottleneck wasn't the model. The culprit was a stateful MCP tool called analytics_query that held pagination cursors, mid-aggregation values, and filter chains in instance memory between calls. Cloudflare Workers...

articles.title: I stopped checking my Modal dashboard because my agent does it for me
articles.summary: I used to spend way too much time context-switching between Cursor, my terminal, and the Modal web dashboard. articles.title: We Just Published 27 MCP Servers to the Official Registry — Here's How to Use Them
articles.summary: The Official MCP Registry is the canonical source of truth for Model Context Protocol servers. As of today, all 27 NexGenData MCP servers are live there, under the namespace com.thenextgennexus. articles.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
articles.summary: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
